test.name: Set record_persona_id on assertions from a record_search result
test.description: Extract assertions from a record_search result handed over by search-records. Because the record came from record_search (logged as log_001 with a results sidecar), each assertion must carry record_persona_id — the GedcomX person id of its persona within the result's gedcomx — and the persisted record_id must be the sidecar's stored form (extraction_append canonicalizes any submitted ARK form — full URL, bare ark:/61903/1:1:<id>, or entity id — to the sidecar's recordId, here the full URL). The focus persona's record_persona_id must equal the result's primaryId.

judge_context: search-records already logged this search as log_001 (a record_search entry whose sidecar holds the gedcomx). The skill should reference that existing log_entry_id on the source and assertions — it should NOT call research_log_append to create a new log entry for a record another skill already logged

Every new assertion must carry record_persona_id — the GedcomX person id (P1, P2, or P3) of the persona it is about. The PERSISTED record_id must end up as the sidecar's stored form (the full arkUrl, https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:MXHY-TP4): extraction_append canonicalizes whatever ARK form was submitted, so do NOT penalize the tool-call argument for being a bare ark:/61903/1:1:MXHY-TP4 or entity id — grade the persisted value in research.json
Assertions about the focus persona (Patrick) must have record_persona_id equal to the result's primaryId (P1)
Relationship assertions from the 1850 census are indirect evidence — the 1850 census does not state relationships explicitly

Informant: for name/age/birthplace, use 'unknown household member' with reasoning (e.g., 'likely a parent' for a child) and proximity 'household_member'. For residence, 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness' is acceptable. Use informant_bias_notes to explain reasoning.
